etymology.description: 亻(亻, "person") as semantic + 主(主, "master") as phonetic -> to live
etymology.interpretation: The character 住(住) (zhù, "to live") combines the semantic component 亻(亻) (rén, "person") with the phonetic component 主(主) (zhǔ, "master"). The person radical indicates the action done by a person, specifically residing or living in a place, while 主(主) provides the sound. This combination suggests a person staying or residing, reinforcing the concept of dwelling or living.

examples.chinese: 我住在北京(我住在北京)。
他们住在山上(他們住在山上)，每天都能看到美丽的日出(每天都能看到美麗的日出)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen zhù zài shān shàng, měitiān dōu néng kàn dào měilì de rìchū.
examples.english: They live on the mountain and can see the beautiful sunrise every day.

examples.chinese: 他曾经住在英国(他曾經住在英國)，后来搬到了日本(後來搬到了日本)。
examples.pinyin: Tā céngjīng zhù zài Yīngguó, hòulái bān dào le Rìběn.
examples.english: He used to live in the UK, then moved to Japan.
